mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate wage income and consumer spending capacity, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with positive PAYEMS changes do not produce significantly higher average 20-day forward returns than months with negative changes over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over a rolling three-month window signals deteriorating labor-market slack and collapsing household demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes where UNRATE rises more than 0.5pp in 3 months fail to produce negative average 60-day returns, or the mean return is not statistically lower than the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating industrial production and simultaneous payroll growth captures broad-based real demand expansion that is not fully reflected when either series is considered alone, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: A two-factor model using INDPRO and PAYEMS jointly does not improve the rank correlation with 60-day forward returns compared to the best single-factor model using either series alone over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is constrained near the zero lower bound, an uptick in consumer prices signals reflation and reduces real debt burdens rather than triggering tighter policy, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25%, month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL are not followed by positive average 5-day returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,400 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Observations where HOUST is below 1400 do not produce negative average 5-day forward returns, or the hit rate of negative returns is below 45% over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the federal funds rate raise risk-free discount rates and corporate borrowing costs, mechanically reducing the present value of future equity cash flows and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with rising FEDFUNDS are followed by positive average 20-day returns over any 3-year rolling window, or the correlation between monthly FEDFUNDS changes and 20-day forward returns is positive and significant.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contracting month-over-month indicates negative goods-sector momentum and inventory accumulation, prompting downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with negative INDPRO changes do not produce significantly negative average 20-day forward returns compared to months with positive changes over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Nominal retail sales exceeding $620 billion reflect resilient consumer expenditure that supports top-line revenue growth for broad-based corporations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Episodes with RSAFS above 620000 do not produce positive average 60-day returns, or the excess return over the unconditional mean is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).
